pr_body: Adds a search_users view that looks up users by name.
author: contributor

files.patch:
@@ -1 +1,7 @@
 import sqlite3
+
+
+def search_users(request):
+    query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '%s'" % request.GET["q"]
+    conn = sqlite3.connect("app.db")
+    return conn.execute(query).fetchall()

triage: {"file_tiers": {"pygoat/introduction/views.py": "deep"}, "overall_risk": "high", "relevant_dimensions": ["security"], "reasoning": "PyGoat: deep-review the search view introducing a raw SQL query."}
analyze: {"findings": [{"finding_type": "sql_injection", "evidence_tier": "observed", "query_match_id": "python.function_definition", "trace_path": null, "title": "SQL injection via string-formatted query in search_users", "description": "search_users() builds a SQL query by %-formatting the untrusted request.GET['q'] value straight into the WHERE clause, then executes it. An attacker controls the query. Use a parameterized query instead.", "evidence": "    query = \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '%s'\" % request.GET[\"q\"]", "line_start": 5, "line_end": 5, "trace_candidates": []}]}
synthesize: PyGoat true-positive: one CRITICAL SQL-injection finding -- search_users() formats untrusted request input directly into a SQL query.
